articles.title: 'Smoke was everywhere': How Bangkok's pub inferno killed 27 - 10 points
articles.description: Tragedy struck at Rong Beer Na Lat Phrao pub, where a fierce fire resulted in the deaths of twenty-seven people. Victims found themselves ensnared in the pub's bathrooms as they tried to escape. Smoke filled the venue quickly, contributing to the catastrophe. Emergency responders faced significant hurdles when attempting to rescue those inside. Authorities have launched an investigation to uncover what led to this heartbreaking event.

articles.title: Entry to 136-yr-old Kolkata airport mosque stopped indefinitely
articles.description: Kolkata airport authorities have indefinitely stopped entry to the Bankra mosque. Security concerns were raised by the Bureau of Civil Aviation Security regarding Aadhaar card access. Previous repairs to the approach road are now complete for devotees. Airport officials await new instructions on a potential pass system. Local leaders are seeking clarity and discussing community action.
articles.full_text: Tamaghna Banerjee, a reporter from Kolkata, covers crime, aviation, human rights and politics. He has a keen interest in human interest and rural reporting. He has done his postgraduation in journalism and mass communication. He has a total of 14 years in journalism. Read More

articles.title: Rohit Sharma undertakes match-simulation in nets ahead of ODI series opener
articles.description: Rohit Sharma put in an extensive match-simulation batting session during India's training at Edgbaston ahead of the first ODI against England, while Virat Kohli sharpened his game against both pace and spin before sharing valuable insights with young pacer Gurnoor Brar as preparations gathered momentum.
articles.full_text: Rohit Sharma put in an extensive match-simulation batting session during India's training at Edgbaston ahead of the first ODI against England, while Virat Kohli sharpened his game against both pace and spin before sharing valuable insights with young pacer Gurnoor Brar as preparations gathered momentum.

articles.title: Jeff Bezos' ex-wife MacKenzie Scott donates to US youth mental health non-profit
articles.description: MacKenzie Scott has donated twenty million dollars to Active Minds. This significant contribution is the largest in the nonprofit's history. Active Minds focuses on youth mental health awareness and reducing stigma. The unrestricted funds will expand programs and support young leaders. This donation occurs amid growing concerns about youth mental health.

articles.title: Quote of the day by Michael Jordan: "I can accept failure, everyone fails at something. But…" - the life lesson that proves regret often lasts longer than failure
articles.description: Michael Jordan's quote highlights that trying is more important than fearing failure. He accepted failure as a normal part of any worthwhile endeavor. Jordan's own career demonstrates numerous setbacks and relentless effort. The greatest obstacle is often the fear of beginning something new. Every setback offers an opportunity for improvement and learning.

articles.title: Europe death toll tops 10,000 during late-June heatwave; scientists blame human-caused climate change
articles.description: Europe recorded over ten thousand excess deaths during a late June heatwave. Older populations, aged sixty-five and above, were most severely impacted by the extreme heat. Scientists link this heatwave's intensity and frequency to human-caused climate change. France and Belgium reported very high excess mortality during the final week of June.

articles.title: Zelenskyy ousts Ukrainian PM as part of broader cabinet reshuffle
articles.description: Ukrainian Prime Minister Yulia Svyrydenko has stepped down from her position. President Zelenskyy announced fresh government changes and a new role for the former premier. Svyrydenko expressed pride in leading the government during difficult times. She stated that she discussed next steps with President Zelenskyy. The President indicated a shift in the nation's political strategy.

articles.title: Chaos returns to Middle East: Oil prices jump 4% as US-Iran exchange strikes
articles.description: Oil prices surged sharply as renewed military action between Iran and the United States escalated tensions. Fresh strikes expanded to Qatar and the United Arab Emirates over the weekend. This escalation has clouded the future of the interim US-Iran agreement signed last month. Market participants remain cautious about how the developing situation could unfold. Iran's Ministry of Foreign Affairs issued a strong condemnation of the latest US military strikes.

articles.title: Auto lenders bet on embedded finance
articles.description: Auto financiers are pushing for embedded finance and digital credit integration. This shift aims to make car purchases more seamless for customers. Traditional installment loans still dominate the Indian auto finance market. Dealers seek more transparent wholesale funding and quicker rate reductions. Robust used-vehicle funding and smoother reconciliation are also needed.

articles.title: After two years, pharma volumes pick up pace
articles.description: Domestic pharmaceutical retail growth shows strong signs of demand-led expansion. Volume growth in June exceeded three percent, a significant positive shift. Industry growth outlook is now revised upwards to over eleven percent this year. Higher sales of lipid-lowering and cardiac therapies led the market's recent surge. Chronic therapies continue to outpace the market, driven by lifestyle diseases.
articles.full_text: A business journalist with around two decades of experience tracking key consumer-focussed sectors like consumer durables, retail, consumer goods, aviation, automobiles and advertising, as well as economic ministries of the Union government. Now, writes primarily on pharmaceuticals and healthcare, and on issues of consumer interest. Besides also looks at trends that are shaping consumer behaviour and the broad consumer landscape. \nYou can follow Rupali on Twitter@Rupalijee. articles.title: Government weighs cybersecurity framework for IoT devices
articles.description: India is considering a cybersecurity framework for internet-connected devices. This initiative aims to enhance security standards for smart meters and other IoT products. The government is exploring measures for stricter security and certification requirements. This move follows a similar mandate for internet-enabled CCTV cameras. The focus remains on ensuring product security regardless of manufacturing origin.

articles.title: ODI tickets worth Rs 52.36 lakh went unsold: Khanna
articles.description: "Tickets worth Rupees 52,36,000 were unsold. Why all the tickets were not sold-out? DDCA started online ticket sale quite late that too without proper advertisement," Khanna claimed in a media release.

articles.title: BSEB issues D.El.Ed. institute list, sets July 15 deadline for error correction through email
articles.description: Bihar School Examination Board (BSEB) has published the 2026 list of recognised and affiliated D.El.Ed. institutions and directed colleges to verify their details. Institutes must report any discrepancies exclusively through email by 5:00 PM on July 15, 2026, along with supporting documents. Postal submissions and late representations will not be accepted by the board.
articles.full_text: Bihar School Examination Board (BSEB) has published the 2026 list of recognised and affiliated D.El.Ed. institutions and directed colleges to verify their details. Institutes must report any discrepancies exclusively through email by 5:00 PM on July 15, 2026, along with supporting documents. Postal submissions and late representations will not be accepted by the board.

articles.title: UP Co-operative Bank admit card shortly as UPCISB prepares for July 20 recruitment examinations; check details here
articles.description: The UP Co-operative Institutional Service Board is expected to release the UP Co-operative Bank Admit Card 2026 shortly before the recruitment examinations scheduled from July 20 to July 22. Candidates will be able to download the hall ticket from the official website using their registration details. The recruitment covers both Special and General Recruitment Drives, while applicants must carry the prescribed documents and follow the examination schedule issued for the various posts.

articles.title: MTET 2026 hall ticket released ahead of July 24 Meghalaya teacher eligibility examination; download here
articles.description: The Directorate of Educational Research and Training, Meghalaya, has released the MTET 2026 admit card for candidates appearing in the teacher eligibility examination on July 24. The hall ticket can be downloaded using the registration number and password through the official portal. The examination will be conducted in offline mode for Paper I and Paper II. Candidates facing download issues may contact DERT or visit their district DIET centre for assistance with obtaining the admit card.

Candidates facing download issues may contact DERT or visit their district DIET centre for assistance with obtaining the admit card.

articles.title: WBJEE Counselling 2026 Round 2 registration begins today; seat allotment on July 21: Check details here
articles.description: The West Bengal Joint Entrance Examinations Board has opened Round 2 registration and choice filling for WBJEE Counselling 2026 from July 13 to July 17. The Round 2 seat allotment result will be announced on July 21, with admissions and document verification scheduled through July 23. Seat allocation will depend on candidates' merit, preferences, eligibility, and seat availability.

The Round 2 seat allotment result will be announced on July 21, with admissions and document verification scheduled through July 23. Seat allocation will depend on candidates' merit, preferences, eligibility, and seat availability.

articles.title: CM Chandrababu Naidu visits Pawan Kalyan in hospital
articles.description: Andhra Pradesh Chief Minister Chandrababu Naidu visited Pawan Kalyan in Mumbai. The Deputy Chief Minister underwent successful surgery on his right shoulder. Doctors confirmed the operation was completed successfully and recovery is progressing well. Pawan Kalyan has been advised three weeks of physiotherapy for rehabilitation. He is reportedly eager to resume his official responsibilities soon.

articles.title: Dhanush's 'Asuran' writer Poomani passes away at 79
articles.description: Tamil literary legend Poomani, a Sahitya Akademi Award winner, passed away at seventy-nine. His novel 'Vekkai' was adapted into the successful film 'Asuran', reaching a wider audience. Poomani also directed the movie 'Karuvelam Pookkal' for the NFDC. His authentic narratives depicted the lives and sufferings of common people. Poomani's literary creations will continue to inspire readers and filmmakers.

articles.title: Jaafar Jackson's 'Michael' crosses USD 1 billion globally
articles.description: The Michael biopic has surpassed one billion dollars in global box office revenue, making it the first-ever biopic to achieve this remarkable feat. Since its release in April, the film has shattered multiple records. Director Antoine Fuqua expressed heartfelt appreciation for the worldwide audience's support, highlighting the film's tribute to Michael Jackson's lasting influence on music and culture.

articles.title: Salman Khan sports a cowboy look with a turquoise stone pendant - Watch
articles.description: Recently, Bollywood icon Salman Khan stepped out with a rugged cowboy-inspired appearance outside a dubbing studio in Bandra. Fans are eagerly anticipating his upcoming role in the war drama 'Mathrubhumi: May War Rest in Peace', slated for release in 2026. In addition, he is also busy with the film 'SVC 63', directed by Vamshi Paidipally, which is expected to hit theatres in 2027.
